Operation 'testUrlRewritingRules' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too generic and doesn't clarify that this is a validation/testing operation. Added context about what the operation accepts (rules in POST body), what it returns (validation results with matches and replacements), and clarified that this is a non-destructive test operation. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation versus actually saving rules.

data.current_summary: Match and replace parts of a URL based on rules passed in POST data
data.current_description: Match and replace parts of a URL based on rules passed in POST data.
data.summary_suggestion: Validate URL rewriting rules against test URLs
data.description_suggestion: Validates URL rewriting rules by testing them against provided URLs. Accepts URL rewriting rules in the POST request body and returns the results of applying those rules to test URLs, including matched patterns and replacement outcomes. This is a validation operation with no persistent side effects - rules are not saved to the project.
data.reasoning: Original description is too generic and doesn't clarify that this is a validation/testing operation. Added context about what the operation accepts (rules in POST body), what it returns (validation results with matches and replacements), and clarified that this is a non-destructive test operation. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation versus actually saving rules.

message: Operation 'getProjectUrlsAggs' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, unclear_purpose, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original summary 'Project Query aggregator' is too technical and vague. Description lacks clarity about what 'aggregator' means and what the operation actually returns. Added context about accepting multiple queries, executing against completed analyses, the request schema structure, and what the operation returns (aggregated results). This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation for bulk URL data analysis versus individual URL queries.

data.current_summary: Project Query aggregator
data.current_description: Project Query aggregator. It accepts multiple queries that will be executed on all completed analyses in the project
data.summary_suggestion: Execute aggregation queries on project URLs
data.description_suggestion: Executes multiple aggregation queries against all completed analyses in the project. Accepts an array of URL aggregation queries in the POST request body (UrlsAggsQueries schema) and returns aggregated results across the specified analyses. Each query is executed independently on the project's URL data, enabling bulk analysis of metrics, statistics, and patterns across crawl results.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Project Query aggregator' is too technical and vague. Description lacks clarity about what 'aggregator' means and what the operation actually returns. Added context about accepting multiple queries, executing against completed analyses, the request schema structure, and what the operation returns (aggregated results). This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation for bulk URL data analysis versus individual URL queries.

message: Operation 'getPageRankLost' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, unclear_purpose, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. data.current_summary: Lost pagerank
data.current_description: Lost pagerank
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve lost PageRank metrics for analysis
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves PageRank loss metrics for the specified analysis. Returns aggregated data showing the amount of PageRank lost across crawled URLs, helping identify SEO impact from changes or issues. Useful for understanding authority distribution changes.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are identical and extremely vague. They don't explain what the operation does, what it returns, or why an AI agent would use it. Added context about the operation's purpose (retrieving PageRank loss metrics), what it returns (aggregated data), and its business value for SEO analysis.

data.current_summary: Get global information of the sitemaps found (sitemaps indexes, invalid sitemaps urls, etc
data.current_description: Get global information of the sitemaps found (sitemaps indexes, invalid sitemaps urls, etc.)
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a comprehensive report of all sitemaps discovered during the analysis, including sitemap indexes, invalid sitemap URLs, coverage statistics, and validation status. Returns aggregated metrics about sitemap health and completeness.
data.reasoning: Summary is incomplete (cut off mid-sentence) and description lacks clarity about what is returned. Added complete summary, clarified what metrics are included in the report, and specified that it returns aggregated data for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Sample list of URLs which were found in your sitemaps but outside of the
data.current_description: Sample list of URLs which were found in your sitemaps but outside of the crawl perimeter defined for the project, for instance domain/subdomain or protocol (HTTP/HTTPS) not allowed in the crawl settings.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve sitemap URLs outside crawl configuration
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a sample list of URLs found in sitemaps but outside the project's crawl perimeter (e.g., disallowed domains, subdomains, or protocols). Returns paginated URL samples that violate crawl configuration settings, useful for identifying sitemap configuration issues.
data.reasoning: Summary is incomplete (cut off). Description explains the concept well but lacks clarity about what is returned (sample list format, pagination). Enhanced summary to be complete and actionable, and clarified the response format.

data.current_summary: Top domains
data.current_description: Top domains
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve top linking domains for analysis
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the top external domains linking to URLs in the analysis, ranked by link frequency or authority. Returns aggregated link data showing which domains are the primary sources of inbound links, useful for backlink analysis and link profile assessment.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are identical and extremely vague. data.current_summary: Top subddomains
data.current_description: Top subddomains
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve top linking subdomains for analysis

data.current_summary: List of Orphan URLs
data.current_description: List of Orphan URLs. URLs which generated visits from the selected source according to Google Analytics data, but were not crawled with by the Botify crawler (either because no links to them were found on the website, or because the crawler was not allowed to follow these links according to the project settings).   For a search engine (medium: origanic; sources: all, aol, ask, baidu, bing, google, naver, yahoo, yandex) or a social network (medium: social; sources: all, facebook, google+, linkedin, pinterest, reddit, tumblr, twitter)
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve URLs with Google Analytics traffic but not crawled
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves orphan URLs that generated visits according to Google Analytics data but were not discovered or crawled by Botify. These URLs may be unreachable due to missing internal links or crawler restrictions. Requires specifying the traffic medium (organic search or social) and source (e.g., google, facebook). Returns a list of orphan URL objects with visit metrics. Use this to identify missed optimization opportunities and crawl coverage gaps.
data.reasoning: Summary was too vague. Description was overly long with formatting issues and unclear parameter documentation. Improved summary clarifies the operation's purpose. Reorganized description for clarity, moved parameter details to be more concise, and added context about why orphan URLs matter for SEO optimization.
